pairs.display: It was a great success and the Emperor presented him with a cloak, to issue from the city.
pairs.genji_half: It was a great success and the Emperor presented him with a cloak,
pairs.quijote_half: to issue from the city.
pairs.genji_source:
It was a great
success and the Emperor presented him with a cloak, which everyone
said was a most unusual honour.
pairs.quijote_source:
of some runners, that
seemed to issue from the city.

pairs.display: At this moment Aoi’s father came by and there passed a tube of tin carefully adjusted and concealed from sight.
pairs.genji_half: At this moment Aoi’s father came by and
pairs.quijote_half: there passed a tube of tin carefully adjusted and concealed from sight.
pairs.genji_source:
At this moment Aoi’s father came by and began to discuss the unusual
success of the recent festivities.
pairs.quijote_source:
Through the entire cavity in the pedestal, table,
throat and neck of the bust or figure, there passed a tube of tin
carefully adjusted and concealed from sight.

pairs.display: Meanwhile Princess Aoi became strangely distraught, and asked him what he was doing.
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile Princess Aoi became strangely distraught,
pairs.quijote_half: and asked him what he was doing.
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile Princess Aoi became strangely distraught, and it seemed at
times as though some hostile spirit had entered into her.
pairs.quijote_source: He approached one man, among others, and asked him what he was doing.

pairs.display: She brooded constantly upon the nature of her own feelings towards Aoi, and lowered the yard with a prodigious rattle.
pairs.genji_half: She brooded constantly upon the nature of her own feelings towards Aoi,
pairs.quijote_half: and lowered the yard with a prodigious rattle.
pairs.genji_source:
She brooded constantly upon the
nature of her own feelings towards Aoi, but could discover in herself
nothing but intense unhappiness.
pairs.quijote_source:
At this instant they struck the awning and lowered
the yard with a prodigious rattle.

pairs.display: It seemed to her that she had been in a large magnificent room, and how does that one man who goes along there whistling dare to whip so many?
pairs.genji_half: It seemed to her that she had been in a large magnificent room,
pairs.quijote_half: and how does that one man who goes along there whistling dare to whip so many?
pairs.genji_source:
It seemed to
her that she had been in a large magnificent room, where lay a girl
whom she knew to be the Princess Aoi.
pairs.quijote_source:
What can those wretches have
done to be so whipped; and how does that one man who goes along there
whistling dare to whip so many?

pairs.display: Suddenly Aoi was seized with the same choking-fit that by her beauty she made death itself look beautiful.
pairs.genji_half: Suddenly Aoi was seized with the same choking-fit
pairs.quijote_half: that by her beauty she made death itself look beautiful.
pairs.genji_source:
Suddenly Aoi was seized
with the same choking-fit as before and was soon in a desperate
condition.
pairs.quijote_source:
Upon the
catafalque was seen the dead body of a damsel so lovely that by her
beauty she made death itself look beautiful.

pairs.display: Though the masses for Aoi’s soul were now over, so that in this way the vessel could not escape them.
pairs.genji_half: Though the masses for Aoi’s soul were now over,
pairs.quijote_half: so that in this way the vessel could not escape them.
pairs.genji_source:
Though the masses for Aoi’s soul were now over, he remained in
retirement till the end of the seven weeks.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
general ordered two to put out to sea while he with the other kept in
shore, so that in this way the vessel could not escape them.

pairs.display: In the space beyond Aoi’s curtained seat, packed away behind a screen, the viceroy of the city was on the shore.
pairs.genji_half: In the space beyond Aoi’s curtained seat, packed away behind a screen,
pairs.quijote_half: the viceroy of the city was on the shore.
pairs.genji_source:
In the space beyond Aoi’s
curtained seat, packed away behind a screen, some thirty gentlewomen
all clad in dark grey weeds were huddled together, forlorn and tearful.
pairs.quijote_source:
The general anchored close in, and perceived that the
viceroy of the city was on the shore.

pairs.display: On New Year’s Day he paid the usual visits of ceremony to his father, but not to expect pardon for his flagrant offence.
pairs.genji_half: On New Year’s Day he paid the usual visits of ceremony to his father,
pairs.quijote_half: but not to expect pardon for his flagrant offence.
pairs.genji_source:
On New Year’s Day he paid the usual visits of ceremony to his
father, to the Emperor and to the Heir Apparent.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
general bade him say what he pleased, but not to expect pardon for his
flagrant offence.

pairs.display: A vast number of Courtiers followed in his train, as their behaviour savoured rather of madness than of bravado.
pairs.genji_half: A vast number of Courtiers followed in his train,
pairs.quijote_half: as their behaviour savoured rather of madness than of bravado.
pairs.genji_source:
A vast number of Courtiers followed in his
train, so that his visit created almost as much bustle and confusion
as that of the Emperor himself.
pairs.quijote_source:
The viceroy,
however, begged him earnestly not to hang them, as their behaviour
savoured rather of madness than of bravado.

pairs.display: True the Emperor had died without betraying that the whole business was a piece of enchantment.
pairs.genji_half: True the Emperor had died without betraying
pairs.quijote_half: that the whole business was a piece of enchantment.
pairs.genji_source:
True the Emperor had died without betraying the least suspicion
concerning the child’s parentage.
pairs.quijote_source:
He fancied that all was a dream, that the whole business was a
piece of enchantment.

pairs.display: In person His Majesty much resembled the late Emperor, and bound not to take up arms for a year.
pairs.genji_half: In person His Majesty much resembled the late Emperor,
pairs.quijote_half: and bound not to take up arms for a year.
pairs.genji_source:
In person His Majesty much resembled the late Emperor,
but he was of a quicker and livelier disposition.
pairs.quijote_source:
Here was his master defeated, and bound not to take
up arms for a year.

pairs.display: They discussed all manner of affairs together, including their Chinese studies, and their eyes were the tongues that declared their pure and happy feelings.
pairs.genji_half: They discussed all manner of affairs together, including their Chinese studies,
pairs.quijote_half: and their eyes were the tongues that declared their pure and happy feelings.
pairs.genji_source:
They discussed all manner of affairs together, including their Chinese
studies, and the Emperor consulted him about the interpretation of
various difficult passages.
pairs.quijote_source:
It was silence that spoke for the
lovers at that moment, and their eyes were the tongues that declared
their pure and happy feelings.

pairs.display: On the anniversary of the Emperor’s death, in addition to the usual ceremonies, many a difficult affair was settled there with the help of favour and bribes.
pairs.genji_half: On the anniversary of the Emperor’s death, in addition to the usual ceremonies,
pairs.quijote_half: many a difficult affair was settled there with the help of favour and bribes.
pairs.genji_source:
On the anniversary of the Emperor’s death, in addition to the usual
ceremonies, he caused the Service of the Eight Recitals to be
celebrated with particular magnificence.
pairs.quijote_source:
Don Antonio offered to arrange the matter at the capital, whither
he was compelled to go on some other business, hinting that many a
difficult affair was settled there with the help of favour and bribes.

pairs.display: Twenty-one white horses were offered to the Emperor on the 7th day, inviting us to break our slumbers by a vigil of some sort.
pairs.genji_half: Twenty-one white horses were offered to the Emperor on the 7th day,
pairs.quijote_half: inviting us to break our slumbers by a vigil of some sort.
pairs.genji_source:
Twenty-one white horses were offered to the Emperor on the 7th
day, and afterwards distributed by him among members of his family.
pairs.quijote_source:
See the calmness of the night,
the solitude of the spot, inviting us to break our slumbers by a vigil of
some sort.

pairs.display: She had no children to look after her and had noise that seemed to spread through all the valleys around.
pairs.genji_half: She had no children to look after her and had
pairs.quijote_half: noise that seemed to spread through all the valleys around.
pairs.genji_source:
She had no children to look after her and had, since
the old Emperor’s death, been living in very bad circumstances.
pairs.quijote_source:
At this point they became aware of a harsh indistinct noise that seemed
to spread through all the valleys around.

pairs.display: The old Emperor on his death-bed warned me of all this, coming towards them some ten men on horseback and four or five on foot.
pairs.genji_half: The old Emperor on his death-bed warned me of all this,
pairs.quijote_half: coming towards them some ten men on horseback and four or five on foot.
pairs.genji_source:
The old Emperor on his death-bed
warned me of all this, but I took no notice, and now I shall suffer
for it.’
pairs.quijote_source:
Then the pair resumed their journey, and as
evening closed in they saw coming towards them some ten men on horseback
and four or five on foot.

pairs.display: This zithern was originally a present from the Emperor Engi and directed their steps to their village.
pairs.genji_half: This zithern was originally a present from the Emperor Engi
pairs.quijote_half: and directed their steps to their village.
pairs.genji_source:
This zithern was
originally a present from the Emperor Engi and has been in my family
for three generations.
pairs.quijote_source:
With this they descended the slope and directed their steps to their
village.

pairs.display: You will remember that the Fifth Princess became seasoned to such work almost from the time they were in swaddling-clothes.
pairs.genji_half: You will remember that the Fifth Princess became
pairs.quijote_half: seasoned to such work almost from the time they were in swaddling-clothes.
pairs.genji_source:
You will
remember that the Fifth Princess became, under the instruction of her
father the Emperor Saga, the most famous performer of her
whole generation.
pairs.quijote_source:
Not you; for that’s a life and a business for
hardy men, bred and seasoned to such work almost from the time they were
in swaddling-clothes.

pairs.display: She spoke with such fierce conviction it’s better to be a knight-errant than a shepherd!
pairs.genji_half: She spoke with such fierce conviction
pairs.quijote_half: it’s better to be a knight-errant than a shepherd!
pairs.genji_source:
She spoke with such fierce conviction that the Emperor was
completely overawed.
pairs.quijote_source:
Why, to make choice of evils, it’s better to be a
knight-errant than a shepherd!

pairs.display: During this time his secret nocturnal excursions were entirely abandoned, and depression were bringing him to his end.
pairs.genji_half: During this time his secret nocturnal excursions were entirely abandoned,
pairs.quijote_half: and depression were bringing him to his end.
pairs.genji_source:
During this time his secret nocturnal excursions
were entirely abandoned, and the Lady of Akashi naturally imagined that
all her fears had now come true.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
doctor’s opinion was that melancholy and depression were bringing him to
his end.

pairs.display: The Emperor was fully determined to resign the Throne to him at the earliest opportunity, so that the housekeeper and niece thought he was going to sleep for ever.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor was fully determined to resign the Throne to him at the earliest opportunity,
pairs.quijote_half: so that the housekeeper and niece thought he was going to sleep for ever.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor was fully
determined to resign the Throne to him at the earliest opportunity,
but should he do so it would be necessary to appoint a regent.
pairs.quijote_source:
They obeyed, and he slept at one stretch, as the
saying is, more than six hours, so that the housekeeper and niece thought
he was going to sleep for ever.

pairs.display: It would be said that he was running after the Lady of Akashi, and let us take to the fields in shepherd’s trim as we agreed.
pairs.genji_half: It would be said that he was running after the Lady of Akashi,
pairs.quijote_half: and let us take to the fields in shepherd’s trim as we agreed.
pairs.genji_source:
It would be said that he was running after the Lady of Akashi, the
old recluse’s daughter.
pairs.quijote_source:
Come, don’t be lazy, but
get up from your bed and let us take to the fields in shepherd’s trim as
we agreed.

pairs.display: Her charm was such that, despite his jealousy, and a faintness coming over him he stretched himself out at full length on the bed.
pairs.genji_half: Her charm was such that, despite his jealousy,
pairs.quijote_half: and a faintness coming over him he stretched himself out at full length on the bed.
pairs.genji_source:
Her charm was such that, despite his
jealousy, the Emperor had grown steadily more attached to her in
the years that had passed.
pairs.quijote_source:
With this he closed his will, and a faintness coming over him he
stretched himself out at full length on the bed.

pairs.display: The princess, Aoi’s mother, for want of thee will be full of evil-doers, no longer in fear of punishment for their misdeeds!
pairs.genji_half: The princess, Aoi’s mother,
pairs.quijote_half: for want of thee will be full of evil-doers, no longer in fear of punishment for their misdeeds!
pairs.genji_source:
The princess, Aoi’s
mother, remained entirely unmoved by the renewed good fortunes of her
husband and family.
pairs.quijote_source:
Oh pride of thy race, honour and glory of all La Mancha,
nay, of all the world, that for want of thee will be full of evil-doers,
no longer in fear of punishment for their misdeeds!

pairs.display: She had indeed left the Emperor’s Palace over fine sands and white pebbles that look like sifted gold and purest pearls.
pairs.genji_half: She had indeed left the Emperor’s Palace
pairs.quijote_half: over fine sands and white pebbles that look like sifted gold and purest pearls.
pairs.genji_source:
She had indeed left the Emperor’s
Palace and now lived in apartments attached to those of the Crown
Prince, her son.
pairs.quijote_source:
Here he sees
a brook whose limpid waters, like liquid crystal, ripple over fine sands
and white pebbles that look like sifted gold and purest pearls.

pairs.display: The Lady of Akashi had been unable to pay her accustomed visit in peace and good-fellowship, for when God sends the dawn, he sends it for all.
pairs.genji_half: The Lady of Akashi had been unable to pay her accustomed visit
pairs.quijote_half: in peace and good-fellowship, for when God sends the dawn, he sends it for all.
pairs.genji_source:
The Lady of Akashi had been unable to pay her accustomed
visit to the Shrine either last autumn or during the spring of this
year.
pairs.quijote_source:
But let nobody play pranks
on me, for either we are or we are not; let us live and eat in peace and
good-fellowship, for when God sends the dawn, he sends it for all.

pairs.display: When she told the Emperor that some one new was coming to see him, for all times are not alike, nor are people always in good humour.
pairs.genji_half: When she told the Emperor that some one new was coming to see him,
pairs.quijote_half: for all times are not alike, nor are people always in good humour.
pairs.genji_source:
When she
told the Emperor that some one new was coming to see him, he listened
very earnestly and attentively.
pairs.quijote_source:
Let
them learn, plague take them, the right way to ask, and beg, and behave
themselves; for all times are not alike, nor are people always in good
humour.

pairs.display: Perhaps an opportunity would occur later on when the Emperor was of an age to perceive warning of coming misfortunes by means of such trivial things as these.
pairs.genji_half: Perhaps an opportunity would occur later on when the Emperor was of an age to perceive
pairs.quijote_half: warning of coming misfortunes by means of such trivial things as these.
pairs.genji_source:
Perhaps an opportunity would occur later
on when the Emperor was of an age to perceive for himself that such a
match was by no means to be despised.
pairs.quijote_source:
With another
Mendoza the salt is spilt on his table, and gloom is spilt over his
heart, as if nature was obliged to give warning of coming misfortunes by
means of such trivial things as these.

pairs.display: Prince Sochi was at once called to the Emperor’s side and immediately he was reminded of those cross-roads where knights-errant used to stop to consider which road they should take.
pairs.genji_half: Prince Sochi was at once called to the Emperor’s side
pairs.quijote_half: and immediately he was reminded of those cross-roads where knights-errant used to stop to consider which road they should take.
pairs.genji_source:
Prince Sochi was at once
called to the Emperor’s side and appointed part-umpire in the coming
contest.
pairs.quijote_source:
He now came to a road branching in four directions, and immediately he
was reminded of those cross-roads where knights-errant used to stop to
consider which road they should take.

pairs.display: Not having previously had any opportunity of showing these sketches to His Majesty, but in the Kingdom of Fez they call the Mudejars Elches, and they are the people the king chiefly employs in war.
pairs.genji_half: Not having previously had any opportunity of showing these sketches to His Majesty,
pairs.quijote_half: but in the Kingdom of Fez they call the Mudejars Elches, and they are the people the king chiefly employs in war.
pairs.genji_source:
Not having previously had any opportunity
of showing these sketches to His Majesty, I took advantage of this
occasion to display them.
pairs.quijote_source:
The Moors of Aragon are called Tagarins in Barbary, and those
of Granada Mudejars; but in the Kingdom of Fez they call the Mudejars
Elches, and they are the people the king chiefly employs in war.

pairs.display: The Lady of Akashi, already awake, for a dolt like me is not bound to see into the thoughts and plots of those vile enchanters.
pairs.genji_half: The Lady of Akashi, already awake,
pairs.quijote_half: for a dolt like me is not bound to see into the thoughts and plots of those vile enchanters.
pairs.genji_source:
The Lady of Akashi, already
awake, kept going to her window and looking out across the sea.
pairs.quijote_source:
But, señora, your excellence must not
therefore think me ill-disposed, for a dolt like me is not bound to see
into the thoughts and plots of those vile enchanters.

pairs.display: The Lady of Akashi had made sure in that other life which is endless and boundless.
pairs.genji_half: The Lady of Akashi had made sure
pairs.quijote_half: in that other life which is endless and boundless.
pairs.genji_source:
The Lady of Akashi had made sure that he
would be there to welcome her.
pairs.quijote_source:
Man’s life alone, swifter than time, speeds onward to its end without any
hope of renewal, save it be in that other life which is endless and
boundless.

pairs.display: The Lady of Akashi took the advice of several other persons who had a reputation and beside them came the fife player, black and sombre like the others.
pairs.genji_half: The Lady of Akashi took the advice of several other persons who had a reputation
pairs.quijote_half: and beside them came the fife player, black and sombre like the others.
pairs.genji_source:
The Lady of Akashi took the advice of several other persons
who had a reputation for sagacity in such matters, and also consulted
various soothsayers and astrologers.
pairs.quijote_source:
As they marched they beat two great
drums which were likewise draped in black, and beside them came the fife
player, black and sombre like the others.

pairs.display: He thought constantly of the country house at Ōi and the broken stitches of his stockings helped them.
pairs.genji_half: He thought constantly of the country house at Ōi
pairs.quijote_half: and the broken stitches of his stockings helped them.
pairs.genji_source:
He thought constantly of the country house at Ōi and of the dull
hours which the Lady of Akashi must be passing there at this season
of festivity.
pairs.quijote_source:
He went to bed with them,
and just like fleas they would not let him sleep or get a moment’s rest,
and the broken stitches of his stockings helped them.

pairs.display: Meanwhile the Lady of Akashi was setting herself to face with resolute calm the dullness and he seated himself upon his bed, and with fear, agitation, and fatigue he fainted away.
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile the Lady of Akashi was setting herself to face with resolute calm the dullness
pairs.quijote_half: and he seated himself upon his bed, and with fear, agitation, and fatigue he fainted away.
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile the Lady of Akashi was setting herself to face with resolute
calm the dullness and monotony of country life.
pairs.quijote_source:
They rubbed him down, fetched him wine and unbound the shields, and he
seated himself upon his bed, and with fear, agitation, and fatigue he
fainted away.

pairs.display: The August visit of the Emperor to her bedside and seeing the havoc the pigs had made with his stores he cursed the drove, and more besides.
pairs.genji_half: The August visit of the Emperor to her bedside
pairs.quijote_half: and seeing the havoc the pigs had made with his stores he cursed the drove, and more besides.
pairs.genji_source:
The August visit
of the Emperor to her bedside and other unusual ceremonies had already
taken place.
pairs.quijote_source:
He awoke, roused himself up, shook himself and stretched his lazy
limbs, and seeing the havoc the pigs had made with his stores he cursed
the drove, and more besides.

pairs.display: The Emperor was in tears, look out for some trusty and pleasant companion to accompany him before he sets out.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor was in tears,
pairs.quijote_half: look out for some trusty and pleasant companion to accompany him before he sets out.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor was in tears, and utterly unable to
control himself even in the presence of a visitor.
pairs.quijote_source:
He who has to make a long journey, will, if he is
wise, look out for some trusty and pleasant companion to accompany him
before he sets out.

pairs.display: Living as I did continually they fled without knowing from whom, at other times they lay in wait, not knowing for what.
pairs.genji_half: Living as I did continually
pairs.quijote_half: they fled without knowing from whom, at other times they lay in wait, not knowing for what.
pairs.genji_source:
Living as I did continually at my father the Emperor’s
side
pairs.quijote_source:
At daybreak they were in one spot, at
dinner-time in another; sometimes they fled without knowing from whom, at
other times they lay in wait, not knowing for what.

pairs.display: This was said with a glance towards the Emperor, the soldier who executes what his captain orders does no less than the captain himself who gives the order.
pairs.genji_half: This was said with a glance towards the Emperor,
pairs.quijote_half: the soldier who executes what his captain orders does no less than the captain himself who gives the order.
pairs.genji_source:
This
was said with a glance towards the Emperor, and in loud clear voice,
that the compliment might not be missed.
pairs.quijote_source:
For, if the
truth is to be told, the soldier who executes what his captain orders
does no less than the captain himself who gives the order.

pairs.display: Tamakatsura was four years old but it was all preaching in the desert and hammering cold iron.
pairs.genji_half: Tamakatsura was four years old
pairs.quijote_half: but it was all preaching in the desert and hammering cold iron.
pairs.genji_source:
Tamakatsura was four years old when she made
the journey to Tsukushi.
pairs.quijote_source:
They strove by all the
means in their power to divert him from such an unlucky scheme; but it
was all preaching in the desert and hammering cold iron.

pairs.display: The old lady prayed fervently to every Buddha at the corners of the market-places, or in the closets of palaces.
pairs.genji_half: The old lady prayed fervently to every Buddha
pairs.quijote_half: at the corners of the market-places, or in the closets of palaces.
pairs.genji_source:
The old lady prayed
fervently to every Buddha and God that some way might present itself of
taking Tamakatsura to Kyōto.
pairs.quijote_source:
But this maiden will not bear to be
handled, nor dragged through the streets, nor exposed either at the
corners of the market-places, or in the closets of palaces.

pairs.display: The story of the beautiful Tamakatsura was one of the handsomest and most graceful youths that could be imagined.
pairs.genji_half: The story of the beautiful Tamakatsura
pairs.quijote_half: was one of the handsomest and most graceful youths that could be imagined.
pairs.genji_source:
The story of the beautiful
Tamakatsura and of the secret deformity which prevented her marriage
soon reached Tayū’s ears.
pairs.quijote_source:
While he was engaged in
conversation with me, they brought him word that in company with me was
one of the handsomest and most graceful youths that could be imagined.

pairs.display: So Tamakatsura replied, and in utter despair she flung herself face downward upon her seat, we were discovered, and the result was what you have seen.
pairs.genji_half: So Tamakatsura replied, and in utter despair she flung herself face downward upon her seat,
pairs.quijote_half: we were discovered, and the result was what you have seen.
pairs.genji_source:
So Tamakatsura replied, and in utter
despair she flung herself face downward upon her seat, where she lay
motionless for many hours.
pairs.quijote_source:
We sighted this
shore last night, and knowing nothing of these galleys, we were
discovered, and the result was what you have seen.

pairs.display: He obtained an interview with the old priest asking their opinion as to the condition, quality, goodness or badness of the wine.
pairs.genji_half: He obtained an interview with the old priest
pairs.quijote_half: asking their opinion as to the condition, quality, goodness or badness of the wine.
pairs.genji_source:
He obtained an interview with the old
priest and arranged that Tamakatsura should be allowed to visit the
Temple.
pairs.quijote_source:
They gave the two of them some wine out of a cask, to
try, asking their opinion as to the condition, quality, goodness or
badness of the wine.

pairs.display: To Tamakatsura he sent, among other gifts, his judge in order to put the sentence pronounced into execution.
pairs.genji_half: To Tamakatsura he sent, among other gifts,
pairs.quijote_half: his judge in order to put the sentence pronounced into execution.
pairs.genji_source:
To Tamakatsura he sent, among other gifts, a close-fitting dress
with a pattern of mountain-kerria woven upon a plain red background.
pairs.quijote_source:
But at last he
untied him, and gave him leave to go look for his judge in order to put
the sentence pronounced into execution.

pairs.display: It was felt, however, that this misfortune was to some extent remedied by the arrival and there, like flies on honey, thoughts came crowding upon him and stinging him.
pairs.genji_half: It was felt, however, that this misfortune was to some extent remedied by the arrival
pairs.quijote_half: and there, like flies on honey, thoughts came crowding upon him and stinging him.
pairs.genji_source:
It was
felt, however, that this misfortune was to some extent remedied by
the arrival of his handsome natural daughter (for so Tamakatsura was
regarded by the world at large).
pairs.quijote_source:
He was
under the shade of a tree, as has been said, and there, like flies on
honey, thoughts came crowding upon him and stinging him.

pairs.display: But Tamakatsura was instantly terror-stricken; of this there could be no doubt, had his rage allowed him, it is my belief he would have laughed.
pairs.genji_half: But Tamakatsura was instantly terror-stricken; of this there could be no doubt,
pairs.quijote_half: had his rage allowed him, it is my belief he would have laughed.
pairs.genji_source:
But Tamakatsura was instantly terror-stricken; of this there could be
no doubt, for she was trembling from head to foot.
pairs.quijote_source:
He saw him rising and falling in the air with
such grace and nimbleness that, had his rage allowed him, it is my belief
he would have laughed.

pairs.display: Startled by the sudden glow of light, Tamakatsura snatched up her fan and they would have stripped off his stockings if his greaves had not prevented them.
pairs.genji_half: Startled by the sudden glow of light, Tamakatsura snatched up her fan
pairs.quijote_half: and they would have stripped off his stockings if his greaves had not prevented them.
pairs.genji_source:
Startled by the sudden glow of light, Tamakatsura
snatched up her fan and buried her face behind it, not before Sochi
had caught an enchanting glimpse of her beauty.
pairs.quijote_source:
They then stripped him of a jacket
that he wore over his armour, and they would have stripped off his
stockings if his greaves had not prevented them.

pairs.display: In another moment Tamakatsura’s maids, summoned hastily to the scene, gave such a stroke that they knocked the guts out of it and scattered the leaves about.
pairs.genji_half: In another moment Tamakatsura’s maids, summoned hastily to the scene,
pairs.quijote_half: gave such a stroke that they knocked the guts out of it and scattered the leaves about.
pairs.genji_source:
In another moment Tamakatsura’s maids, summoned
hastily to the scene, had detached the strange lantern and carried it
somewhere out of sight.
pairs.quijote_source:
To one
of them, a brand-new, well-bound one, they gave such a stroke that they
knocked the guts out of it and scattered the leaves about.

pairs.display: But the greatest enthusiast of all was Lady Tamakatsura, who would rise at daybreak and bring me to the vessel.
pairs.genji_half: But the greatest enthusiast of all was Lady Tamakatsura, who would rise at daybreak
pairs.quijote_half: and bring me to the vessel.
pairs.genji_source:
But the greatest
enthusiast of all was Lady Tamakatsura, who would rise at daybreak and
spend the whole day absorbed in reading or copying out romances.
pairs.quijote_source:
You can carry me away from there by night without any danger,
and bring me to the vessel.

pairs.display: Judged as an episode, thought Tamakatsura, crowned with the laurels and crowns of victory and triumph.
pairs.genji_half: Judged as an episode, thought Tamakatsura,
pairs.quijote_half: crowned with the laurels and crowns of victory and triumph.
pairs.genji_source:
Judged as an
episode, thought Tamakatsura, her own escape from the violence of Tayū
was quite as exciting.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
fair woman who is a woman of honour, and whose husband is poor, deserves
to be crowned with the laurels and crowns of victory and triumph.

pairs.display: Never in her life had she seen so many handsome faces the one the cynosure of knight-errantry, the other the star of squirely fidelity!
pairs.genji_half: Never in her life had she seen so many handsome faces
pairs.quijote_half: the one the cynosure of knight-errantry, the other the star of squirely fidelity!
pairs.genji_source:
Never in her life had she seen
so many handsome faces and fine clothes; but the Emperor could hold
his own.
pairs.quijote_source:
Fair be the fortunes of such a
master and such a servant, the one the cynosure of knight-errantry, the
other the star of squirely fidelity!

pairs.display: So long as she continued to live quietly and without any seasoning, but for all that toothsome and pleasant.
pairs.genji_half: So long as she continued to live quietly
pairs.quijote_half: and without any seasoning, but for all that toothsome and pleasant.
pairs.genji_source:
So long as
she continued to live quietly in the New Palace the question of
Tamakatsura’s clan-rights was not likely to be raised.
pairs.quijote_source:
Nor was there any lack of olives, dry, it is true, and without any
seasoning, but for all that toothsome and pleasant.

pairs.display: So far as I can make out, her misfortune will have been good fortune, and my defeat a most happy triumph.
pairs.genji_half: So far as I can make out,
pairs.quijote_half: her misfortune will have been good fortune, and my defeat a most happy triumph.
pairs.genji_source:
So far as I can make out, she is just the sort of person
his Majesty is looking for.
pairs.quijote_source:
If she returns to her lost shape (and it cannot be
but that she will) her misfortune will have been good fortune, and my
defeat a most happy triumph.

pairs.display: But it is not intended that this shall continue, beseeching him to take care of his health and treat himself to a suitable diet.
pairs.genji_half: But it is not intended that this shall continue,
pairs.quijote_half: beseeching him to take care of his health and treat himself to a suitable diet.
pairs.genji_source:
But it is not intended that this shall continue, and Lady
Tamakatsura seems to me just the right person to put things upon a more
sensible footing.
pairs.quijote_source:
And so they took their leave of him,
recommending and beseeching him to take care of his health and treat
himself to a suitable diet.

pairs.display: The 16th of this month, being the first day of the Higan Festival, she spared herself the staircase, and flung them down out of the window.
pairs.genji_half: The 16th of this month, being the first day of the Higan Festival,
pairs.quijote_half: she spared herself the staircase, and flung them down out of the window.
pairs.genji_source:
The 16th of
this month, being the first day of the Higan Festival, seemed
particularly favourable for the holding of Tamakatsura’s Initiation
Ceremony.
pairs.quijote_source:
They were handed to her, and as there were many of them, she spared
herself the staircase, and flung them down out of the window.

pairs.display: Having protested that his message was of a very private nature, having regard to their sufferings and not to their rascalities.
pairs.genji_half: Having protested that his message was of a very private nature,
pairs.quijote_half: having regard to their sufferings and not to their rascalities.
pairs.genji_source:
Having protested that
his message was of a very private nature, he was at last allowed to
address Tamakatsura through the curtains of the southern window.
pairs.quijote_source:
It
only concerns them to aid them as persons in need of help, having regard
to their sufferings and not to their rascalities.

pairs.display: True, he was seldom at home; but she fell at his feet the moment she saw him.
pairs.genji_half: True, he was seldom at home; but
pairs.quijote_half: she fell at his feet the moment she saw him.
pairs.genji_source:
True, he was seldom at home; but apart from
his courtship of Tamakatsura herself, his time seemed to be devoted
entirely to practical affairs.
pairs.quijote_source:
She found him pacing the patio of his
house, and, perspiring and flurried, she fell at his feet the moment she
saw him.

pairs.display: Such at least was the pretext which he used and how wide are the bounds of thy pleasant jurisdiction!
pairs.genji_half: Such at least was the pretext which he used
pairs.quijote_half: and how wide are the bounds of thy pleasant jurisdiction!
pairs.genji_source:
Such at least was the pretext which he used for
delaying Tamakatsura’s departure.
pairs.quijote_source:
power of flattery,
how far-reaching art thou, and how wide are the bounds of thy pleasant
jurisdiction!

pairs.display: The Emperor had himself suggested her application and let me go in my coach with my feet off the ground.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor had himself suggested her application
pairs.quijote_half: and let me go in my coach with my feet off the ground.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor had
himself suggested her application for the vacant post.
pairs.quijote_source:
But let them tramp through the mud, and
let me go in my coach with my feet off the ground.

pairs.display: Henceforward the image of Tamakatsura haunted for where there is deep love there will never be overmuch boldness.
pairs.genji_half: Henceforward the image of Tamakatsura haunted
pairs.quijote_half: for where there is deep love there will never be overmuch boldness.
pairs.genji_source:
Henceforward the image of Tamakatsura haunted his mind
more constantly than ever.
pairs.quijote_source:
They did not embrace each other, for where
there is deep love there will never be overmuch boldness.

pairs.display: The Emperor will in any case think that and stationed them, each on the spot where he was to stand.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor will in any case think that
pairs.quijote_half: and stationed them, each on the spot where he was to stand.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor will in any case think that I
have behaved in a very inconsistent way.
pairs.quijote_source:
The master of the ceremonies apportioned the sun to them,
and stationed them, each on the spot where he was to stand.

pairs.display: When Lady Tamakatsura is living of dust which rises yonder?
pairs.genji_half: When Lady Tamakatsura is living
pairs.quijote_half: of dust which rises yonder?
pairs.genji_source:
When Lady Tamakatsura is living here everything
will be easier....
pairs.quijote_source: Seest thou that cloud of dust which rises yonder?

pairs.display: His own secret prayer was that she might at least recover her reason and a great deal more of which there is nothing in all the other books.
pairs.genji_half: His own secret prayer was that she might at least recover her reason
pairs.quijote_half: and a great deal more of which there is nothing in all the other books.
pairs.genji_source:
His own secret prayer was that
she might at least recover her reason for a sufficient length of time
to permit of his installing Tamakatsura in the house.
pairs.quijote_source:
Here knights eat and sleep, and die in their beds, and make their wills
before dying, and a great deal more of which there is nothing in all the
other books.

pairs.display: At dusk he set out and began spearing them with as much spirit and intrepidity as if he were transfixing mortal enemies in earnest.
pairs.genji_half: At dusk he set out
pairs.quijote_half: and began spearing them with as much spirit and intrepidity as if he were transfixing mortal enemies in earnest.
pairs.genji_source: At dusk he set out as usual for Tamakatsura’s rooms.
pairs.quijote_source:
So saying, he dashed into the midst of the squadron of ewes, and began
spearing them with as much spirit and intrepidity as if he were
transfixing mortal enemies in earnest.

pairs.display: She blushed crimson as she read it, and was just thinking how she could possibly reply, taking him to be some one who was on their side.
pairs.genji_half: She blushed crimson as she read it, and was just thinking how she could possibly reply,
pairs.quijote_half: taking him to be some one who was on their side.
pairs.genji_source:
She blushed crimson as she read it,
and was just thinking how she could possibly reply, when the Emperor
himself came that way.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
members of the troop received him into the midst of them, taking him to
be some one who was on their side.

pairs.display: His Majesty expressed in a very good-tempered and hoarse voice but with great courtesy.
pairs.genji_half: His Majesty expressed in a very good-tempered
pairs.quijote_half: and hoarse voice but with great courtesy.
pairs.genji_source:
His Majesty expressed in a very good-tempered and
considerate way his regret that she had decided not to live permanently
at the Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
Approaching them, the youth greeted them in a harsh and hoarse voice but
with great courtesy.

pairs.display: He was not best pleased, very much surprised to see him there, and made great efforts to dismount.
pairs.genji_half: He was not best pleased,
pairs.quijote_half: very much surprised to see him there, and made great efforts to dismount.
pairs.genji_source:
He was not best pleased,
for he feared that the Emperor would regard so sudden a flight as very
discourteous.
pairs.quijote_source:
He, astonished at the stranger’s words and behaviour,
looked at him attentively, and at length recognised him, very much
surprised to see him there, and made great efforts to dismount.

pairs.display: The young Emperor too, though their acquaintance had been so brief, for they would fetch him away in spite of himself.
pairs.genji_half: The young Emperor too, though their acquaintance had been so brief,
pairs.quijote_half: for they would fetch him away in spite of himself.
pairs.genji_source:
The young Emperor too, though their acquaintance had been so brief,
could not get Tamakatsura out of his head.
pairs.quijote_source:
The licentiate in reply told him not to be uneasy, for
they would fetch him away in spite of himself.

pairs.display: After her recovery Tamakatsura continued to administer the business of the Bedchamber from her husband’s house, and his reason seemed to be deserting him little by little.
pairs.genji_half: After her recovery Tamakatsura continued to administer the business of the Bedchamber from her husband’s house,
pairs.quijote_half: and his reason seemed to be deserting him little by little.
pairs.genji_source:
After her recovery Tamakatsura continued to administer the business
of the Bedchamber from her husband’s house, and seemed to show no
intention of ever again appearing at the Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
He knew not what to think, or say, or do, and
his reason seemed to be deserting him little by little.

pairs.display: Fortunately she remembered a recipe that drew more than one tear from some of the listeners, particularly the women, who are by nature tender-hearted and compassionate.
pairs.genji_half: Fortunately she remembered a recipe that
pairs.quijote_half: drew more than one tear from some of the listeners, particularly the women, who are by nature tender-hearted and compassionate.
pairs.genji_source:
Fortunately
she remembered a recipe that had been invented by Minamoto no
Kintada with the help of notes inherited from the Emperor Uda.
pairs.quijote_source:
These words, and the touching
earnestness with which she uttered them, drew more than one tear from
some of the listeners, particularly the women, who are by nature
tender-hearted and compassionate.

pairs.display: It was open to the Lady of Akashi to follow on foot, and all of sufficient age to make choice of a profession.
pairs.genji_half: It was open to the Lady of Akashi to follow on foot,
pairs.quijote_half: and all of sufficient age to make choice of a profession.
pairs.genji_source:
It
was open to the Lady of Akashi to follow on foot, and as far as she
herself was concerned she would have been ready enough to do so.
pairs.quijote_source:
My father had three, all sons, and all of sufficient
age to make choice of a profession.

pairs.display: All these arrangements had been devised by Tamakatsura herself, of the blest to dwell.
pairs.genji_half: All these arrangements had been devised by Tamakatsura herself,
pairs.quijote_half: of the blest to dwell.
pairs.genji_source:
All these arrangements had been devised by Tamakatsura
herself, who had a particular gift for the happy ordering of such small
elegancies.
pairs.quijote_source: In the bright mansions of the blest to dwell.

pairs.display: She brought her own furniture with her, who could tell us where we were.
pairs.genji_half: She brought her own furniture with her,
pairs.quijote_half: who could tell us where we were.
pairs.genji_source:
She brought her own furniture with her, just as is done by a
new arrival at the Emperor’s Palace.
pairs.quijote_source: long we must see some one who could tell us where we were.

pairs.display: Again and again it seemed as though the end were come, seen her by any chance?
pairs.genji_half: Again and again it seemed as though the end were come,
pairs.quijote_half: seen her by any chance?
pairs.genji_source:
Again and again it seemed as though the end were come,
and Genji lived in so continual an agony of suspense that during this
whole time he never once went near Nyosan’s rooms.
pairs.quijote_source: And have you ever seen her by any chance?

pairs.display: But Kaoru had about him something refined, distinguished, and what clime and quarter of the world he is in.
pairs.genji_half: But Kaoru had about him something refined, distinguished,
pairs.quijote_half: and what clime and quarter of the world he is in.
pairs.genji_source:
But Kaoru had about him something refined, distinguished, that would
have marked him out among a hundred other well-born children.
pairs.quijote_source:
He must be an astronomer, so as to know by the stars how many hours
of the night have passed, and what clime and quarter of the world he is
in.

pairs.display: Now Genji found himself and whenever Love passed in front of the castle he shot his arrows up at it, while Interest broke gilded pellets against it.
pairs.genji_half: Now Genji found himself
pairs.quijote_half: and whenever Love passed in front of the castle he shot his arrows up at it, while Interest broke gilded pellets against it.
pairs.genji_source:
Now Genji found himself (not without some surprise at
the degree of condescension to which his misfortunes had brought him)
welcoming the fellow as an equal, and commiserating with him upon his
plight.
pairs.quijote_source:
All
then mingled together, forming chains and breaking off again with
graceful, unconstrained gaiety; and whenever Love passed in front of the
castle he shot his arrows up at it, while Interest broke gilded pellets
against it.

pairs.display: Indeed, he worried a thousand times more over Nyosan, who had already received numerous flattering offers, who, when the humour took him, would abuse her and call her clown-bred and clodhopper’s daughter and spinning wench.
pairs.genji_half: Indeed, he worried a thousand times more over Nyosan, who had already received numerous flattering offers,
pairs.quijote_half: who, when the humour took him, would abuse her and call her clown-bred and clodhopper’s daughter and spinning wench.
pairs.genji_source:
Indeed, he worried a
thousand times more over Nyosan, who had already received numerous
flattering offers, than over the other sisters who could scarcely
muster one wretched suitor apiece.
pairs.quijote_source:
A fine thing it
would be, indeed, to marry our Maria to some great count or grand
gentleman, who, when the humour took him, would abuse her and call her
clown-bred and clodhopper’s daughter and spinning wench.

pairs.display: That evening they had left the Palace together, but when they parted Genji (Chūjō had noticed) did not fire on him.
pairs.genji_half: That evening they had left the Palace together, but when they parted Genji (Chūjō had noticed)
pairs.quijote_half: did not fire on him.
pairs.genji_source:
That evening they had left the Palace together, but when
they parted Genji (Chūjō had noticed) did not either go in the
direction of the Great Hall nor back to his own palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
The members of the band, however, were satisfied with
seeing him take to flight, and did not fire on him.

pairs.display: The works that Genji had written out were all masterpieces of the past, some Chinese, some Japanese; answer he had to make to what his opponent said.
pairs.genji_half: The works that Genji had written out were all masterpieces of the past, some Chinese, some Japanese;
pairs.quijote_half: answer he had to make to what his opponent said.
pairs.genji_source:
The works that
Genji had written out were all masterpieces of the past, some Chinese,
some Japanese; some written in cursive, some in full script; they
constituted indeed an astonishing display of versatile penmanship.
pairs.quijote_source:
Seeing this the great governor asked the creditor what answer he had to
make to what his opponent said.

pairs.display: His own old nurse was a sister of Nyosan’s nurse with a better and everlasting spouse.
pairs.genji_half: His own old nurse was a sister of Nyosan’s nurse
pairs.quijote_half: with a better and everlasting spouse.
pairs.genji_source:
His own old nurse was a sister of Nyosan’s nurse (the mother of Kojijū,
whose assistance had proved so fruitless before).
pairs.quijote_source:
Claudia told him
she meant to go to a monastery of which an aunt of hers was abbess, where
she intended to pass her life with a better and everlasting spouse.

pairs.display: Or again, that a creature such as Lady Fujitsubo should vanish so soon, for the captains had already paid down their sixty.
pairs.genji_half: Or again, that a creature such as Lady Fujitsubo should vanish so soon,
pairs.quijote_half: for the captains had already paid down their sixty.
pairs.genji_source:
Or
again, that a creature such as Lady Fujitsubo should vanish so soon,
while this absurd grandam, even in her younger days totally devoid
of charm or intelligence, should be left behind!
pairs.quijote_source:
The
regent’s lady ordered one of her servants to give the eighty crowns that
had been assessed as her share at once, for the captains had already paid
down their sixty.

pairs.display: He would have liked to proclaim his new-born but there was no possibility of doing so, for they hid themselves among the hundreds of others that were following them.
pairs.genji_half: He would have liked to proclaim his new-born
pairs.quijote_half: but there was no possibility of doing so, for they hid themselves among the hundreds of others that were following them.
pairs.genji_source:
He would have
liked to proclaim his new-born child as Heir Apparent in place of
Kōkiden’s son.
pairs.quijote_source:
His conductors tried to
punish the audacity of the boys, but there was no possibility of doing
so, for they hid themselves among the hundreds of others that were
following them.

pairs.display: There was a great bustle and it was clear that they would overcome him, as you have seen to-day.
pairs.genji_half: There was a great bustle and it was clear that they would
pairs.quijote_half: overcome him, as you have seen to-day.
pairs.genji_source:
There
was a great bustle and it was clear that they would soon be starting
out to fetch Princess Kōkiden back from the Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
But this did not quench my desire to meet him again and overcome
him, as you have seen to-day.

pairs.display: These we recognized as Shii no Shōshō and Uchūben, the father with tears, the daughter with bashfulness.
pairs.genji_half: These we recognized as Shii no Shōshō and Uchūben,
pairs.quijote_half: the father with tears, the daughter with bashfulness.
pairs.genji_source:
These we recognized as Shii
no Shōshō and Uchūben, so there is little doubt that the carriages
belonged to Princess Kōkiden.
pairs.quijote_source:
Ricote
and his daughter came out to welcome him, the father with tears, the
daughter with bashfulness.

pairs.display: She knew with what feelings Kōkiden regarded her and was aware that the fair ladies of the inn gave the fair damsel a cordial welcome.
pairs.genji_half: She knew with what feelings Kōkiden regarded her and was aware that
pairs.quijote_half: the fair ladies of the inn gave the fair damsel a cordial welcome.
pairs.genji_source:
She knew with what feelings
Kōkiden regarded her and was aware that her existence at a Court
dominated by this woman’s arbitrary power could not be otherwise than
unhappy.
pairs.quijote_source:
In short, the Judge made his entrance in a state of bewilderment, as well
with what he saw as what he heard, and the fair ladies of the inn gave
the fair damsel a cordial welcome.

pairs.display: During the late Emperor’s lifetime Kōkiden had or guards, or bolts that can protect a young girl better than her own modesty.
pairs.genji_half: During the late Emperor’s lifetime Kōkiden had
pairs.quijote_half: or guards, or bolts that can protect a young girl better than her own modesty.
pairs.genji_source:
During the late Emperor’s lifetime Kōkiden had been obliged to behave
with a certain restraint.
pairs.quijote_source:
Her father watched over her and she watched over herself; for there are
no locks, or guards, or bolts that can protect a young girl better than
her own modesty.

pairs.display: The Lady Kōkiden still showed the same unrelenting hostility, it is so; ah! woe is me!
pairs.genji_half: The Lady Kōkiden still showed the same unrelenting hostility,
pairs.quijote_half: it is so; ah! woe is me!
pairs.genji_source:
The Lady
Kōkiden still showed the same unrelenting hostility, finding at every
turn some means to inconvenience or humiliate her.
pairs.quijote_source:
If
the solitude these mountains promise deceives me not, it is so; ah! woe
is me!

pairs.display: Henceforward the whole government of the country fell into the hands of a single family, for if you were to hear this you would go mad with delight.
pairs.genji_half: Henceforward the whole government of the country fell into the hands of a single family,
pairs.quijote_half: for if you were to hear this you would go mad with delight.
pairs.genji_source:
Henceforward the whole government of the country fell into the hands of
a single family, that of Kōkiden’s father, the Minister of the Right.
pairs.quijote_source:
Hold your peace, señor; for if you were to hear this you would go mad
with delight.

pairs.display: Next came the death of Kōkiden’s father, and her handmaid, and the promise she had given him to inform him matters of serious importance.
pairs.genji_half: Next came the death of Kōkiden’s father,
pairs.quijote_half: and her handmaid, and the promise she had given him to inform him matters of serious importance.
pairs.genji_source:
Next came the death of Kōkiden’s father, the Grand Minister of the
Right.
pairs.quijote_source:
He went at once to see
Camilla, and tell her, as he did, all that had passed between him and her
handmaid, and the promise she had given him to inform him matters of
serious importance.

pairs.display: They say that his present Majesty, the Emperor Ryōzen, taking him by the hand, found that it was cold, and saw that he was dead.
pairs.genji_half: They say that his present Majesty, the Emperor Ryōzen,
pairs.quijote_half: taking him by the hand, found that it was cold, and saw that he was dead.
pairs.genji_source:
They say that his present Majesty, the
Emperor Ryōzen, is the image of you; but I don’t believe a word of it.
pairs.quijote_source:
Having
first called to him without receiving any answer, his host approached
him, and taking him by the hand, found that it was cold, and saw that he
was dead.

pairs.display: After performing before the ex-Emperor the dancers had been summoned what a lot of things you know!
pairs.genji_half: After performing before the ex-Emperor the dancers had been summoned
pairs.quijote_half: what a lot of things you know!
pairs.genji_source:
After
performing before the ex-Emperor the dancers had been summoned to give
a second display in front of Kōkiden’s apartments.
pairs.quijote_source:
Devil take you for a
knight-errant, what a lot of things you know!

pairs.display: He would indeed long ago have entered a monastery, for they would listen to him willingly.
pairs.genji_half: He would indeed long ago have entered a monastery,
pairs.quijote_half: for they would listen to him willingly.
pairs.genji_source:
He would indeed long ago have entered a
monastery, had not his mother Lady Kōkiden, masterful as ever in her
extreme old age, obstinately opposed such a step.
pairs.quijote_source:
They all bade him say what he liked, for they would listen to him
willingly.

pairs.display: Her mother, Genji’s nurse, had afterwards married the governor of Echizen and had gone with him to his province, and there is not a drop of vinegar to be had in the whole village.
pairs.genji_half: Her mother, Genji’s nurse, had afterwards married the governor of Echizen and had gone with him to his province,
pairs.quijote_half: and there is not a drop of vinegar to be had in the whole village.
pairs.genji_source:
Her
mother, Genji’s nurse, had afterwards married the governor of Echizen
and had gone with him to his province, so the girl when she was not at
the Palace lived chiefly at her father’s.
pairs.quijote_source:
There are no olives this year, and there is not a drop
of vinegar to be had in the whole village.

pairs.display: Perhaps some other time when I am not in such a hurry.... Let me close your window for he vanquished me and unhorsed me, and so my plan failed.
pairs.genji_half: Perhaps some other time when I am not in such a hurry.... Let me close your window
pairs.quijote_half: for he vanquished me and unhorsed me, and so my plan failed.
pairs.genji_source:
Perhaps some other time when I am not in such a
hurry.... Let me close your window for you,’ and with that she
rejoined Genji, giving the princess no encouragement to play any more.
pairs.quijote_source:
But fate
ordered it otherwise, for he vanquished me and unhorsed me, and so my
plan failed.

pairs.display: From this enigmatic reply Chūjō deduced that Genji had been in communication of some kind with the lady by forced and rambling verbiage.
pairs.genji_half: From this enigmatic reply Chūjō deduced that Genji had been in communication of some kind with the lady
pairs.quijote_half: by forced and rambling verbiage.
pairs.genji_source:
From this enigmatic reply Chūjō deduced that Genji
had been in communication of some kind with the lady and he was
slightly piqued by the fact that she had shown a preference between
them.
pairs.quijote_source:
Then the
love-thoughts of the heart clothed themselves simply and naturally as the
heart conceived them, nor sought to commend themselves by forced and
rambling verbiage.

pairs.display: Chūjō, faithful to his promise, did not mention the affair to anyone, not even to his sister, for out of a hundred, ninety-nine will turn out cross and contrary.
pairs.genji_half: Chūjō, faithful to his promise, did not mention the affair to anyone, not even to his sister,
pairs.quijote_half: for out of a hundred, ninety-nine will turn out cross and contrary.
pairs.genji_source:
Chūjō, faithful to his promise, did not mention the affair to
anyone, not even to his sister, but kept it as a weapon of
self-defence should Genji ever preach high morality to him again.
pairs.quijote_source:
To be sure most of those one
finds do not end as pleasantly as one could wish, for out of a hundred,
ninety-nine will turn out cross and contrary.

pairs.display: Fujitsubo’s relations were all members of the Imperial family by way of which the government had been conferred upon him.
pairs.genji_half: Fujitsubo’s relations were all members of the Imperial family
pairs.quijote_half: by way of which the government had been conferred upon him.
pairs.genji_source:
Fujitsubo’s relations were
all members of the Imperial family and Genji, to whom he might
have looked for help owing to his affiliation with the Minamoto clan,
unfortunately showed no aptitude for political intrigue.
pairs.quijote_source:
They informed him that it was called the island of Barataria,
either because the name of the village was Baratario, or because of the
joke by way of which the government had been conferred upon him.

pairs.display: So he pleaded, and he was supported by her ladies who told her that Prince Genji was her own dear Lord and where there is niggardliness there can be no liberality.
pairs.genji_half: So he pleaded, and he was supported by her ladies who told her that Prince Genji was her own dear Lord
pairs.quijote_half: and where there is niggardliness there can be no liberality.
pairs.genji_source:
So he pleaded, and he was
supported by her ladies who told her that Prince Genji was her own
dear Lord and that she ought not to be so proud and stiff with him.
pairs.quijote_source:
After all, where envy reigns
virtue cannot live, and where there is niggardliness there can be no
liberality.

pairs.display: So great were the risks he had run that for some time afterwards Genji found himself wishing Fujitsubo’s prudence had a stronger flavour of cordovan.
pairs.genji_half: So great were the risks he had run that for some time afterwards Genji found himself wishing Fujitsubo’s prudence
pairs.quijote_half: had a stronger flavour of cordovan.
pairs.genji_source:
So great were the risks he had run that for some time afterwards
Genji found himself wishing Fujitsubo’s prudence and reserve were
more commonly practised, and at such times he almost applauded her
unkindness.
pairs.quijote_source:
The first said the wine had
a flavour of iron, the second said it had a stronger flavour of cordovan.

pairs.display: Genji was indeed much disquieted; he saluted them and passed on without stopping.
pairs.genji_half: Genji was indeed much disquieted;
pairs.quijote_half: he saluted them and passed on without stopping.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji
was indeed much disquieted; but he contrived on all such occasions to
conceal his discomfiture.
pairs.quijote_source:
When he came up to them, he saluted them and passed on
without stopping.

pairs.display: He was already beginning to breathe more freely when, to crown his satisfaction, drowsiness began to come over them, and they dropped asleep on their very table and tablecloth.
pairs.genji_half: He was already beginning to breathe more freely when, to crown his satisfaction,
pairs.quijote_half: drowsiness began to come over them, and they dropped asleep on their very table and tablecloth.
pairs.genji_source:
He
was already beginning to breathe more freely when, to crown his
satisfaction, very early on the morning of the next day a messenger
from Prince Genji arrived at the house on the hill.
pairs.quijote_source:
At length, the wine
having come to an end with them, drowsiness began to come over them, and
they dropped asleep on their very table and tablecloth.

pairs.display: Should Genji now by any chance succeed where the devotion of years had brought no reward, plotting thine own dishonour, devising thine own ruin.
pairs.genji_half: Should Genji now by any chance succeed where the devotion of years had brought no reward,
pairs.quijote_half: plotting thine own dishonour, devising thine own ruin.
pairs.genji_source:
Should Genji now by any chance
succeed where the devotion of years had brought no reward, he
would certainly feel that he had treated his gentleman very badly and
suffer the discomfort of remorse.
pairs.quijote_source:
Bethink thee thou art working against
thyself, plotting thine own dishonour, devising thine own ruin.

pairs.display: The child was indeed a most exquisite creature, and the young nurse asked me if what they said about the youth was true.
pairs.genji_half: The child was indeed a most exquisite creature, and the young nurse
pairs.quijote_half: asked me if what they said about the youth was true.
pairs.genji_source:
The child was indeed a
most exquisite creature, and the young nurse felt, from the moment it
was presented to her, that Genji’s care and anxiety on its behalf were
by no means ill-bestowed.
pairs.quijote_source:
The king
immediately ordered him to be brought before him that he might see him,
and asked me if what they said about the youth was true.

pairs.display: She told her father of Genji’s decision, and this time at any rate the old man had no cause to judge and no one to be judged.
pairs.genji_half: She told her father of Genji’s decision, and this time at any rate the old man had
pairs.quijote_half: no cause to judge and no one to be judged.
pairs.genji_source:
She told her father of Genji’s decision, and this
time at any rate the old man had good cause for that mixture of joy and
weeping to which he was at all times prone.
pairs.quijote_source:
Arbitrary law had not yet established itself in
the mind of the judge, for then there was no cause to judge and no one to
be judged.

pairs.display: Seeing him sigh heavily and go about muttering to himself, Murasaki knew that a conversation as droll as that which passed between their masters was serious.
pairs.genji_half: Seeing him sigh heavily and go about muttering to himself, Murasaki knew that
pairs.quijote_half: a conversation as droll as that which passed between their masters was serious.
pairs.genji_source:
Seeing him sigh heavily and go about muttering
to himself, Murasaki knew that his love for Yūgao had been no mere
boyish fancy, but an affair that had stirred his nature to its depths.
pairs.quijote_source:
With this the two squires withdrew to one side, and between them there
passed a conversation as droll as that which passed between their masters
was serious.

pairs.display: She was also terrified lest her father should hear that Genji never came to see her; there love songs are heard, here despairing elegies.
pairs.genji_half: She was also terrified lest her father should hear that Genji never came to see her;
pairs.quijote_half: there love songs are heard, here despairing elegies.
pairs.genji_source:
She was also terrified
lest her father should hear that Genji never came to see her; for she
had got it into her head that Suzaku too would at once think she had
done something wicked.
pairs.quijote_source:
Here one shepherd is sighing, there another is lamenting; there love
songs are heard, here despairing elegies.

pairs.display: Genji listened without making any comment; and it inflamed him all the more.
pairs.genji_half: Genji listened without making any comment;
pairs.quijote_half: and it inflamed him all the more.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji listened without making any comment; but he perfectly well
understood the meaning of the dream.
pairs.quijote_source:
Lothario observed all this, and it inflamed him
all the more.

pairs.display: That Genji should mourn was well enough; an end that came of a thoughtless beginning.
pairs.genji_half: That Genji should mourn was well enough;
pairs.quijote_half: an end that came of a thoughtless beginning.
pairs.genji_source:
That
Genji should mourn was well enough; but what right had Yūgiri to this
grievous pain?
pairs.quijote_source:
This was the end of all three, an end that
came of a thoughtless beginning.

pairs.display: When all the boxes were duly arranged on their stands, they would take him away whether he liked it or not.
pairs.genji_half: When all the boxes were duly arranged on their stands,
pairs.quijote_half: they would take him away whether he liked it or not.
pairs.genji_source:
When all the
boxes were duly arranged on their stands, the Emperor’s own ladies
took up their places, some with Lady Chūjō’s supporters, some with the
opposing side.
pairs.quijote_source:
The servants pressed him, saying that most certainly they would
not return without him, and that they would take him away whether he
liked it or not.

pairs.display: It is about Nyosan and the humours of those you take after!
pairs.genji_half: It is about Nyosan
pairs.quijote_half: and the humours of those you take after!
pairs.genji_source: It is about Nyosan that I am chiefly troubled.
pairs.quijote_source:
A plague on your humours and the humours of those you take
after!

pairs.display: While he spoke she kept on asking herself who he was, he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.
pairs.genji_half: While he spoke she kept on asking herself who he was,
pairs.quijote_half: he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.
pairs.genji_source:
While he spoke she kept on asking herself who
he was, and it was only gradually that it dawned upon her he could not
very well be any one but Kashiwagi.
pairs.quijote_source:
The governor on this asked him if he had any money in silver about him;
he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.

pairs.display: It appalled the Lady of Akashi to discover what manifold varieties of suffering and gentleness and modesty abide with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a woman.
pairs.genji_half: It appalled the Lady of Akashi to discover what manifold varieties of suffering
pairs.quijote_half: and gentleness and modesty abide with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a woman.
pairs.genji_source:
It appalled the Lady of Akashi to
discover what manifold varieties of suffering one can be called upon
to endure at one and the same time.
pairs.quijote_source:
In short, as Camilla is the essence of all beauty, so is she
the treasure-house where purity dwells, and gentleness and modesty abide
with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a
woman.

pairs.display: On the third day of the first month, being a day of the Rat, while the living who have been rewarded may be summed up with three figures.
pairs.genji_half: On the third day of the first month, being a day of the Rat,
pairs.quijote_half: while the living who have been rewarded may be summed up with three figures.
pairs.genji_source:
On the third day of the first month, being a day of
the Rat, Tamakatsura determined to take upon herself the customary
presentation of spring shoots.
pairs.quijote_source:
No doubt you will reply that there can be no comparison, that the dead
cannot be numbered, while the living who have been rewarded may be summed
up with three figures.

pairs.display: The secrecy was not however as well maintained as he could have desired; they conducted him into another room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.
pairs.genji_half: The secrecy was not however as well maintained as he could have desired;
pairs.quijote_half: they conducted him into another room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.
pairs.genji_source:
The secrecy was
not however as well maintained as he could have desired; even Suzaku,
in his secluded apartments, heard the story and determined to put his
own collection at the service of Princess Akikonomu.
pairs.quijote_source:
They placed him in the midst of
them, and with much pomp and stateliness they conducted him into another
room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.

pairs.display: Rider after rider again as the procession went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach with an epitaph which was to be to this effect: Beneath the stone before your eyes
pairs.genji_half: Rider after rider again as the procession went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach
pairs.quijote_half: with an epitaph which was to be to this effect: Beneath the stone before your eyes
pairs.genji_source:
Rider after rider again as the procession
went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach and salute her with the
deepest respect.
pairs.quijote_source:
They closed the grave with a heavy
stone until a slab was ready which Ambrosio said he meant to have
prepared, with an epitaph which was to be to this effect:

Beneath the stone before your eyes

pairs.display: The Akashi Princess was also in residence at the New Palace, if they be all ungrateful!
pairs.genji_half: The Akashi Princess was also in residence at the New Palace,
pairs.quijote_half: if they be all ungrateful!
pairs.genji_source:
The Akashi
Princess was also in residence at the New Palace, with her little boy,
Niou, and her second child, also a boy.
pairs.quijote_source:
A plague on all the knights-errant in the
world, if they be all ungrateful!

pairs.display: He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my body-servant, and he talked to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.
pairs.genji_half: He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my body-servant,
pairs.quijote_half: and he talked to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.
pairs.genji_source:
He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my
body-servant, or even recommend him to the Emperor.’
pairs.quijote_source:
They were very
cordially received by him; they asked him after his health, and he talked
to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.

pairs.display: We too, you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji for wisdom, they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.
pairs.genji_half: We too, you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji
pairs.quijote_half: for wisdom, they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.
pairs.genji_source:
We too,
you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji when we were
outwitted by this hasty wedding, of which till the last minute no one
was given the slightest intimation.
pairs.quijote_source:
And if they don’t get me a name for wisdom,
they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.

pairs.display: The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best homespun.
pairs.genji_half: The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering
pairs.quijote_half: while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best homespun.
pairs.genji_source:
The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal
distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering and mingled a
certain feeling of gratification with her father’s perpetual weeping.
pairs.quijote_source:
The rest of it
went in a doublet of fine cloth and velvet breeches and shoes to match
for holidays, while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best
homespun.

pairs.display: The Emperor had been and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor had been
pairs.quijote_half: and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor had been somewhat
nervous about this interview.
pairs.quijote_source:
When the villagers saw the two scholars
so unexpectedly appearing in shepherd’s dress, they were lost in wonder,
and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.

pairs.display: And he made many suggestions that now, even though thou repentest, thou canst not help being mine.
pairs.genji_half: And he made many suggestions
pairs.quijote_half: that now, even though thou repentest, thou canst not help being mine.
pairs.genji_source:
And he made many suggestions for
Tamakatsura’s polite education.
pairs.quijote_source:
It was thy will to make me thine, and
thou didst so follow thy will, that now, even though thou repentest, thou
canst not help being mine.

pairs.display: Ateki of course knew manifest proof of their gallantry and resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their post.
pairs.genji_half: Ateki of course knew
pairs.quijote_half: manifest proof of their gallantry and resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their post.
pairs.genji_source:
Ateki of course knew the secret of
        Tamakatsura’s birth.
pairs.quijote_source:
Of three hundred that remained alive not one was
taken unwounded, a clear and manifest proof of their gallantry and
resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their
post.

pairs.display: His Majesty has an undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position, so likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.
pairs.genji_half: His Majesty has an undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position,
pairs.quijote_half: so likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.
pairs.genji_source:
His Majesty has an
undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position, unless
backed up by the most powerful family connections, is apt to have a
very disagreeable time.
pairs.quijote_source:
Have strong hope
and trust in him, for as he has restored me to my original condition, so
likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.

pairs.display: In his final instructions to Genji concerning the management of public affairs he recurred again but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even the sign of one.
pairs.genji_half: In his final instructions to Genji concerning the management of public affairs he recurred again
pairs.quijote_half: but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even the sign of one.
pairs.genji_source:
In his final instructions to Genji concerning
the management of public affairs he recurred again and again to the
question of the Heir Apparent and the importance of giving him due
protection and advice.
pairs.quijote_source:
In this way, doubling the brays at every step, they made the complete
circuit of the forest, but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even
the sign of one.

pairs.display: The two women exchanged addresses and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji had given her, of her woe resolved.
pairs.genji_half: The two women exchanged addresses and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji had given her,
pairs.quijote_half: of her woe resolved.
pairs.genji_source:
The two women exchanged addresses
and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji
had given her, not far away from his new palace; the others to their
lodgings in the Ninth Ward.
pairs.quijote_source: Thus have the authors of her woe resolved.

pairs.display: And now Genji, who while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping maid-servant, a way of disenchanting!
pairs.genji_half: And now Genji, who while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping maid-servant,
pairs.quijote_half: a way of disenchanting!
pairs.genji_source:
And now Genji, who
while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping
maid-servant, relieved at last by Koremitsu could contain himself no
longer, and suddenly realizing again the terrible thing that had
befallen him he burst into uncontrollable weeping.
pairs.quijote_source: The devil take such a way of disenchanting!

pairs.display: Her attendants had no means of knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened, and some will say they are white and others will say they are black.
pairs.genji_half: Her attendants had no means of knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened,
pairs.quijote_half: and some will say they are white and others will say they are black.
pairs.genji_source:
Her attendants had no means of
knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened, for it had
always been Genji’s habit to go in and out of her room just as he
chose.
pairs.quijote_source:
Say
nothing of this to anyone; for, make thy affairs public, and some will
say they are white and others will say they are black.

pairs.display: Again and again, under pressure from Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right, whereby all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.
pairs.genji_half: Again and again, under pressure from Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right,
pairs.quijote_half: whereby all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.
pairs.genji_source:
Again and again, under pressure from
Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right, he allowed public measures to
be taken of which he did not really in the least approve.
pairs.quijote_source:
And so the night’s
round came to an end, and a couple of days later the government, whereby
all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.
